title: My Peggy's Face.
content:
Tune - "My Peggy's Face."



I.

        My Peggy's face, my Peggy's form,
        The frost of hermit age might warm;
        My Peggy's worth, my Peggy's mind,
        Might charm the first of human kind.
        I love my Peggy's angel air,
        Her face so truly, heav'nly fair,
        Her native grace so void of art,
        But I adore my Peggy's heart.

II.

        The lily's hue, the rose's dye,
        The kindling lustre of an eye;
        Who but owns their magic sway?
        Who but knows they all decay!
        The tender thrill, the pitying tear,
        The gen'rous purpose, nobly dear,
        The gentle look, that rage disarms
        These are all immortal charms.
meaning:
Robert Burns's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "My Peggy's Face."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
